title: Moving from Seattle to Austin: Complete Relocation Guide [2026]
description: Compare cost of living, housing, taxes, and quality of life when moving from Seattle to Austin. Data-driven relocation guide with calculators.
content:
Introduction

Seattle and Austin are two of America’s top tech hubs — but Austin offers dramatically lower costs while maintaining a robust tech scene. If you’re tired of Seattle’s housing prices and rainy winters, Austin might be your answer.

Cost of Living Comparison

Category Seattle Austin Savings
Overall Index 172.1 119.3 -31%
Rent (1BR) $2,350/mo $1,650/mo -$700/mo
Home Price $820,000 $450,000 -45%
Utilities $130/mo $140/mo +8%
Groceries $520/mo $380/mo -27%
Transportation $170/mo $150/mo -12%
Healthcare $400/mo $350/mo -13%

Monthly savings potential: $1,200–$2,000

Salary Calculator

If you make $120,000 in Seattle, you need approximately $83,000 in Austin to maintain the same standard of living.

Salary Equivalence Calculator

Find out what you need to earn to maintain your lifestyle

Housing Market

Seattle

  • Median home: $820,000
  • Median rent (1BR): $2,350/month
  • Price per sq ft: $520

Austin

  • Median home: $450,000
  • Median rent (1BR): $1,650/month
  • Price per sq ft: $285

Austin housing is 45% cheaper than Seattle.

Taxes

Tax Type Seattle Austin
State Income Tax 0% 0%
City Income Tax 0% 0%
Sales Tax 10.25% 8.25%
Property Tax ~0.93% ~1.8%

Both cities have no state income tax — a major advantage.

Climate

Seattle Austin
Summer High 76°F 96°F
Winter Low 37°F 41°F
Sunny Days 152 228
Rainfall 37" 35"

Trade-off: Austin is much sunnier and hotter. Seattle has milder summers but gray winters.

Culture & Lifestyle

What You’ll Miss from Seattle

  • Mountains and hiking (Rainier, Cascades)
  • Coffee culture
  • Mild summer weather
  • Progressive politics
  • Water access (Puget Sound, lakes)

What You’ll Gain in Austin

  • 228 sunny days per year
  • Live music capital of the world
  • BBQ and Tex-Mex
  • More affordable everything
  • No state income tax

Best Neighborhoods in Austin for Seattle Transplants

  1. East Austin — Trendy, artsy, most like Capitol Hill
  2. South Congress — Walkable shops and restaurants
  3. Mueller — Planned community with parks
  4. Downtown Austin — Urban, like South Lake Union

Is It Worth It?

Yes — if you prefer sun to rain.

Both cities have no state income tax and strong tech economies. Austin is significantly cheaper (31% lower cost of living) and much sunnier. The main trade-offs are summer heat and less outdoor scenery.

Frequently Asked Questions

Will my salary go down?

Possibly. Tech salaries in Austin are typically 10–20% lower than Seattle. However, the cost savings more than offset the difference.

Is Austin’s tech scene as good as Seattle’s?

Different but robust. Seattle has Microsoft and Amazon headquarters. Austin has Apple, Google, Meta, Tesla, Dell, and thousands of startups.

What’s the biggest culture shock?

The heat and the driving. Austin summers are extreme (100°F+). And unlike Seattle’s decent public transit, Austin is car-dependent.

How’s the outdoor scene?

Good but different. Austin has Lady Bird Lake, Barton Springs, and Hill Country hiking. But it’s not the Pacific Northwest.

Data Sources

  • Cost of living: Council for Community and Economic Research (C2ER)
  • Housing data: Zillow Research
  • Tax data: Tax Foundation
  • Migration data: IRS Statistics of Income

Last updated: June 28, 2026
